mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing equity valuations. A sustained downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the preceding 3 months signals monetary easing that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.8% as of Nov 2006 reflects recent Fed tightening cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which depresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns. A 0.3 percentage point increase in UNRATE over 3 months triggers this negative signal.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.8% in Nov 2006 near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) reflects consumer demand strength and future corporate revenue expectations. Accelerating retail sales over the preceding 2 months signal robust demand that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity prices adjust to improved earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns following RSAFS acceleration are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic output and capacity utilization. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, it signals supply-side weakness and profit margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite weak industrial production, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction demand. When both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously over 2 months, it indicates broad-based demand weakness that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side recession risk better than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 2% over 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1569K units in Oct 2006, RSAFS at 620214M in Nov 2006
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of joint-decline triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly characterize labor market momentum. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 100K month-over-month while UNRATE rises, it signals labor market inflection and reduced consumer confidence, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth < 100K and UNRATE rises > 0.1pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144894K in Nov 2006, UNRATE at 5.8%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 5%) signals monetary tightening that constrains equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 268.551 (1982-84=100) in Nov 2006, FEDFUNDS at 5.8%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly reflects aggregate demand strength. In late-cycle expansions (GDPC1 growth decelerating to 1.5-2.5% annualized), equity returns compress as growth expectations moderate. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when GDP growth enters this deceleration band.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth between 1.5% and 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19088.064B (2017 dollars) as of Q3 2006
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of deceleration-band triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window in late-cycle regimes.
